formality.description: Swedish historically had a complex formality system but underwent the 'du-reform' (du-reformen) in the 1960s–70s. Modern Swedish uses 'du' universally. 'Ni' as formal address is sometimes used in retail/service but is not standard in software.

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Swedish has common and neuter genders (en/ett). The gender-neutral pronoun 'hen' is officially recognized and widely accepted.

professional.label: Professionell (Professional)
professional.description: Standard for software UI and business communication.
professional.prompt: Professional Swedish. Use du-form (universal in modern Swedish). Clear, modern language.
